title: Infosys to acquire 75% stake in Versent Group from Telstra
detailed_summary: Infosys announced that its wholly owned subsidiary Infosys Singapore Pte. Ltd. will enter into an agreement with Telstra Limited to acquire 75% of the equity share capital in Telstra Purple Pty Ltd, referred to as Versent Group, for AUD 233.25 million in cash consideration, including upfront and deferred consideration and earnouts. Telstra will retain a 25% minority stake, while Infosys will have operational control. The transaction is intended to strengthen Infosys' position in Australia and accelerate AI-enabled cloud and digital transformation solutions for Australian enterprises and government corporations. Versent Group reported FY25 revenues of AUD 211.4 million and has a team of 650 engineers, advisors and strategists across Australia. Completion is expected during the second half of fiscal year 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.

key_facts.fact: Infosys will acquire 75% of the shareholding in Versent Group, a wholly owned subsidiary of Telstra Group.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys and Telstra Announce Joint Venture in Australia

key_facts.fact: Infosys will have operational control, while Telstra will retain a 25% minority stake in Versent Group.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys and Telstra Announce Joint Venture in Australia

key_facts.fact: The transaction is expected to close during the second half of FY 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys and Telstra Announce Joint Venture in Australia

key_facts.fact: Approval is required from the Foreign Investment Review Board of Australia and the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission.
Versent Group reported FY25 revenues of AUD 211.4 million for the fiscal year ending June 30th.
The business is a carve-out and hence the three-year turnover details is not applicable.

processing_notes: The document repeatedly identifies Versent Group as Telstra Purple Pty Ltd and describes the business as an integration of Versent, Epicon, Telstra Purple Digital and associated Cloud Access products. Generic company background, executive quotations, safe harbor language and exchange filing wrapper details were excluded.
